name: Urban League Of Metropolitan Saint Louis Inc - Peter H. Bunce Water Tower Center
description_short: Non-profit organization providing free credit counseling, housing guidance, financial literacy, and community empowerment services to St. Louis residents since 1918.
description_long:
The Urban League of Metropolitan St. Louis, Inc., headquartered at the Peter H. Bunce Water Tower Center, is a longstanding non-profit organization that has served the St. Louis community for over 108 years since its founding in 1918. It operates as the largest affiliate of the National Urban League movement and is dedicated to empowering African Americans and others throughout the region in securing economic self-reliance, social equality, and civil rights.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of free and low-cost services spanning multiple life domains. Their financial services include HUD-certified housing counseling, financial literacy programs (Family Financial Empowerment and Save Our Sisters), and credit wellness events offering free credit reports, FICO scores, and one-on-one credit consultations. Beyond financial services, they provide employment training reaching over 5,000 residents annually, Head Start early childhood education, energy assistance programs (LIHEAP, weatherization, utility assistance), food and clothing support, and community empowerment initiatives.

What distinguishes this organization is its holistic, community-based approach combining financial education with broader social services and advocacy. Their programs target economically disadvantaged populations through multiple integrated services rather than offering a single financial product. The organization maintains strong community partnerships (including Boeing) and operates multiple specialized programs like Save Our Sons, Save Our Sisters, and the Neighborhood Healing Network, demonstrating deep institutional commitment to systemic community change.

As a legitimate non-profit with 108 years of operational history and HUD certification, this organization represents genuine free financial counseling. However, consumers should understand that while credit wellness events and financial literacy are offered free, the organization's primary focus extends well beyond credit repair into broader community empowerment, meaning services may be less specialized than dedicated credit counseling agencies.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling program with experienced counselors
Free credit wellness events including credit reports, FICO scores, and one-on-one consultations
Comprehensive financial literacy programs including Family Financial Empowerment and Save Our Sisters
Over 108 years of operational history and community trust in St. Louis
Integrated services combining employment training, energy assistance, and financial counseling
Employment Services reach over 5,000 local residents annually with free job skills training
Low Income Home Energy Program (LIHEAP) assistance for utility bills
Non-profit organization with no predatory lending or profit motive
cons: Primary focus is broad community empowerment rather than specialized credit repair or monitoring
Services appear concentrated in St. Louis metropolitan area only
Website does not specify counselor credentials beyond HUD certification or NFCC affiliation
Limited information on whether all financial counseling services are truly free or have income requirements
No online or remote counseling options mentioned; appears to require in-person visits
best_for: St. Louis residents seeking free HUD-certified housing counseling and pre-purchase education
Low-income families needing integrated financial literacy combined with employment and energy assistance
Individuals looking for non-profit credit wellness events with free credit reports and personalized guidance
Community members seeking holistic empowerment services beyond credit repair alone
diagnosis: Best for St. Louis residents seeking free, non-profit financial counseling integrated with broader community services and employment support. Main caveat: this is a comprehensive community organization rather than a specialized credit repair or monitoring agency, so those seeking intensive credit dispute services or continuous score monitoring should verify available options before relying on this resource alone.
services: Free credit wellness events with credit reports and FICO scores
One-on-one credit consultations and personalized credit-building strategies
Family Financial Empowerment financial literacy program
Save Our Sisters financial empowerment program
Low Income Home Energy Program (LIHEAP) utility bill assistance
Weatherization Assistance Program for energy efficiency
Employment Services with job skills training and career fairs
Head Start early childhood education program
Utility assistance and food and clothing assistance
Save Our Sons employment and wage opportunity program
Financial literacy and credit education components
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
